_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI Test & Evaluation frameworks.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI evaluation frameworks, including functional performance testing, behavioral testing, red-teaming, fairness evaluation, robustness testing, and safety evaluation.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-004 (RM, cosine: 0.709), RM-S-004 (RM, cosine: 0.645).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.6/10 (raw 0.661). Shared vocabulary: [ai, evaluation, frameworks, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [test] vs WIDAI-only terms [behavioral, fairness, functional, performance, red, robustness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.906 (P=0.948, R=0.868). P exceeds R by 0.080 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 19 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Knowledge of AI Test & Evaluation frameworks." and "Knowledge of AI evaluation frameworks, including functional performance testing," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.819 > STS 0.661 (gap +0.158). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, evaluation, frameworks, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 29.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, evaluation, frameworks, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [test]. WIDAI-only: [behavioral, fairness, functional, performance, red, robustness].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.158 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
